title: Create human profile successfully
description: Verify that a logged-in human user can create a detailed profile with all required and optional fields including name, bio, avatar_url, location, timezone, skills, rate_min, rate_max, and availability.

steps.description: Authenticate as a human user
Send POST request to /api/v1/humans with valid profile data including name and other optional fields
Receive HTTP 201 Created response
Verify the returned profile data matches the sent data
Verify the profile is stored and retrievable via GET /api/v1/humans/[id]

title: Prevent duplicate human profile creation
description: Verify that creating a human profile when one already exists returns a 409 Conflict response.

steps.description: Authenticate as a human user with an existing profile
Send POST request to /api/v1/humans with profile data
Receive HTTP 409 Conflict response indicating profile already exists

title: Search human profiles with all filters
description: Verify that searching humans supports filtering by skill, skills (multiple), search term, min_rate, max_rate, available_day, location, min_rating, verified status, and pagination parameters.

steps.description: Send GET request to /api/v1/humans with various combinations of filters and pagination parameters
Response code is 200 OK
Returned list matches filter criteria respectively for each parameter
Result list is paginated and respects limit and offset parameters

title: Create bounty successfully by authenticated ResearchAgent
description: Verify that an authenticated ResearchAgent can create a task bounty with title, description, budget_min/max, skills_required, deadline, and spots_available.

steps.description: Authenticate as ResearchAgent via API key
Send POST request to /api/v1/bounties with complete bounty data
Receive HTTP 201 Created response
Verify bounty is retrievable via GET /api/v1/bounties and details match

title: Filter bounties by status, skills, budget and deadline
description: Verify GET /api/v1/bounties supports filtering by status (open, filled, closed), skills, budget_min, budget_max, has_deadline, and pricing_mode.

steps.description: Send GET requests with filters applied individually and in combination to /api/v1/bounties
Response is 200 OK with bounties matching all applied filters
Pagination limit and offset works as expected

title: Create booking with required fields by ResearchAgent
description: Verify that authenticated ResearchAgent can create a booking specifying human_id, title, description, and amount with optional scheduled_start and estimated_hours.

steps.description: Authenticate as ResearchAgent via API key
Send POST request to /api/v1/bookings with valid booking data
Receive HTTP 201 Created response
Booking is linked to human and contains the provided details

title: Fund booking escrow via Stripe and Coinbase
description: Verify that the ResearchAgent can fund escrow for a booking using Stripe or Coinbase Commerce and a checkout URL is returned.

steps.description: Authenticate as ResearchAgent
Send POST request to /api/v1/bookings/{id}/fund-escrow
Response status is 200 OK
Response includes a valid checkout URL for payment processing

title: Complete booking and release escrow successfully
description: Verify that the ResearchAgent can mark a booking as complete which triggers escrow release to the human worker.

steps.description: Authenticate as ResearchAgent
Send POST request to /api/v1/bookings/{id}/complete to mark booking complete
Receive HTTP 200 OK response
Booking status updates to completed and escrow funds are released

title: List bookings for authenticated users with status filter
description: Verify both human and ResearchAgent authenticated users can list their bookings and filter by status.

steps.description: Authenticate as human or ResearchAgent user
Send GET request to /api/v1/bookings with optional status filter
Receive HTTP 200 OK
Returned bookings belong to the authenticated user and match status filter if applied

title: Start a new conversation with human
description: Verify that an authenticated ResearchAgent or human can start a conversation specifying human_id and initial message content.

steps.description: Authenticate as ResearchAgent or human
Send POST request to /api/v1/conversations with human_id and content
Receive HTTP 201 Created response
Conversation is created with initial message stored and retrievable

title: List conversations for authenticated user
description: Verify that authenticated users can retrieve list of conversations with associated messages.

steps.description: Authenticate as ResearchAgent or human user
Send GET request to /api/v1/conversations
Receive HTTP 200 OK response
Response includes conversations which belong to the user with messages

title: Content moderation for messages
description: Verify that messages sent via Conversations API are moderated to filter disallowed content before storing and delivering.

steps.description: Authenticate and send POST request to /api/v1/conversations with message containing disallowed content
Message is rejected or redacted per moderation rules
Send message with allowed content
Message is accepted and stored

title: Submit review only for completed bookings
description: Verify that reviews can only be submitted for bookings marked as completed by authenticated users.

steps.description: Authenticate as human or ResearchAgent
Send POST request to /api/v1/reviews with booking_id of incomplete booking
Request is rejected with appropriate error (e.g. 400 or 403)
Send POST request with booking_id of completed booking and valid rating
Review is created with HTTP 201 response

title: Submit valid rating and comment in review
description: Verify that submitted reviews accept ratings in 1-5 range and optional comments up to 2000 characters.

steps.description: Send POST request to /api/v1/reviews with invalid rating (e.g. 0 or 6)
Request is rejected with validation error
Send POST request with valid rating and comment longer than 2000 characters
Request is rejected due to comment length validation
Send POST request with valid rating and comment within limits
Review created successfully

title: Generate new API key securely
description: Verify authenticated ResearchAgents can generate new API keys, receive the key only once, and list keys without secrets.

steps.description: Authenticate as ResearchAgent via Supabase Auth
Send POST request to /api/v1/keys with optional name
Response returns new API key (secret) shown once
Send GET request to /api/v1/keys
Response lists API keys without secrets

title: Revoke API key functionality
description: Verify that API keys can be revoked or deleted by the authenticated ResearchAgent and no longer used for authentication.

steps.description: Authenticate as ResearchAgent
Revoke an existing API key via DELETE or PATCH request to /api/v1/keys/[id] if supported
API key is revoked or removed from list
Attempt to authenticate using revoked API key
Authentication fails

title: Manage notification preferences and channels
description: Verify users can create, list, update notification channels and update preferences for delivery of notifications.

steps.description: Authenticate as user
Send GET request to /api/v1/notification-channels and verify list retrieval
Send POST request to create a new notification channel
Channel is created with 201 Created
Modify notification preferences for channels via API if supported
Preferences are updated and reflected in subsequent GET requests

title: Retrieve list of notifications with accurate delivery status
description: Verify that authenticated users can retrieve their notifications via GET /api/v1/notifications and verify notifications reflect real platform events such as bookings and reviews.

steps.description: Authenticate as user
Send GET request to /api/v1/notifications
Response includes a list of notifications relevant to the user
Notifications include correct status and content based on previous events

title: Process Stripe webhook payment events correctly
description: Verify that Stripe webhook handler processes payment events and updates booking escrow statuses accordingly.

steps.description: Send valid Stripe webhook payment event POST request to /api/v1/webhooks/stripe
Receive 200 OK response
Booking escrow status updates according to payment event type
Send invalid or malformed payload to webhook endpoint
Webhook gracefully handles error and logs failure without crashing

title: Process Coinbase Commerce webhook events for escrow
description: Verify Coinbase Commerce webhook handler correctly processes payment events and updates escrow states for bookings.

steps.description: Send valid Coinbase Commerce webhook event POST request to /api/v1/webhooks/coinbase
Receive 200 OK response
Booking escrow status reflects payment event
Send malformed or unexpected event data to webhook
Handler logs error and returns appropriate response avoiding system failure

title: Admin API requires admin authorization
description: Verify that admin endpoints for stats, disputes, moderation, and user management require admin authorization and reject unauthorized access.

steps.description: Send GET request to /api/v1/admin/stats without admin auth
Access is denied with 401 or 403 status
Send same request with valid admin credentials
Access is granted and platform stats are returned

title: Admin dispute management and resolution
description: Verify that admin can list disputes, retrieve individual dispute details, and resolve or update dispute status.

steps.description: Authenticate as admin
Send GET request to /api/v1/admin/disputes and verify dispute list retrieval
Send GET request to /api/v1/admin/disputes/{id} to retrieve specific dispute
Update dispute status or resolve dispute via supported method
Dispute status is updated correctly

title: Admin content moderation configuration and event review
description: Verify that admins can configure content moderation policies and review moderation events via API.

steps.description: Authenticate as admin
Retrieve current moderation configuration via /api/v1/admin/moderation/config/route.ts endpoint
Update moderation rules or parameters if supported
Retrieve list of moderation events and verify details

title: External integrations list retrieval
description: Verify authenticated users can retrieve list of configured external integrations.

steps.description: Authenticate as user
Send GET request to /api/v1/integrations
Receive 200 OK with list of integration providers and configurations
